goal: todo-sweep: Implement to-do items from docs/status using Opus as orchestrator and Composer 2 for every worker / verifier role; close as many independent open UI / architecture backlog rows as can be parallelized without colliding on shared files (the only open GA-code gate, release-todo §1 / Theme 5 MutationProposal lifecycle, is explicitly out of scope for this sweep because BE undo semantics are unspecified and the slice does not fit a single bounded worker).
summary: todo-sweep: 5 parallel UI / a11y backlog workers, each with a composer-2-fast verifier, plus a docs-status hygiene worker to close the items in docs/status.
acceptanceCriteria: Each implementation worker ships a draft PR that closes its scoped open item end-to-end (code + tests; i18n parity en + zh-CN where applicable).
Each verifier records a strong verification class (unit-test-verified or live-ui-verified) for its target, or surfaces a concrete blocker that the planner can act on.
The docs-status hygiene worker reconciles docs/status/ui-todo.md and docs/status/product-done.md to reflect the merged item closures (no doc/code drift).

tasks.name: fix-project-detail-shell
tasks.scopedGoal: Close ui-todo §5 / Phase 2.5: collapse the duplicate project-detail layout into the main shell, replace the left-aside popover with a real `<Breadcrumb>` element, and fix the stray `5 px` (broken) box-shadow value. Keep the `/projects/:projectId` → `/projects/:projectId/board` redirect intact so existing AI tests still mount the board route.
tasks.brief:
Implementation plan:

1. **`src/pages/projectDetail.tsx`**: replace the dedicated `display: grid; grid-template-columns: 16rem 1fr` shell + left aside + `<ProjectPopover>` with a top-of-content AntD `<Breadcrumb>` (e.g. `Projects / {projectName}`). Keep the existing `useEffect`/redirect that sends `/projects/:projectId` to `/projects/:projectId/board`. Remove the broken `5 px` literal in the `box-shadow` declaration (it silently disables the shadow); use the design-system shadow token from `src/theme/tokens.ts` (`shadow.*`) instead of a hand-rolled rgba string.
2. The breadcrumb must be a real interactive AntD `<Breadcrumb>` (anchor entries via `react-router`'s `<Link>`), not a static span. The breadcrumb's last segment is the project name (visible) with `aria-current="page"`.
3. Move any "Members" / "Projects" affordance that previously lived in the aside into the main header chrome if it lacks a duplicate (do NOT add a duplicate if already present in `src/components/header/index.tsx`); if it's already in the header, simply drop the aside without re-adding it.
4. Add a focused test in `src/__tests__/` that mounts `ProjectDetailPage`, asserts the breadcrumb is present with an accessible name (`Projects` link + current project segment), the `5 px` typo is gone (rendered `box-shadow` does not contain the literal `5 px`), and that visiting `/projects/:id` still redirects to `/projects/:id/board`.
5. i18n: every new visible string is keyed under `microcopy.breadcrumb.*` (or reused if equivalent keys exist); `en.ts` and `zh-CN.ts` both updated. Do not edit `docs/status/**` — the docs-status-hygiene worker owns that file.

Do NOT modify any other component or page beyond what is strictly required to land this slice. Keep the existing test suite green. UI / interactive bug: capture a before/after screenshot or short recording of the project detail route and include the path in your handoff if you have any GUI-driven verification available.
acceptance: `src/pages/projectDetail.tsx` no longer renders a 2-column grid shell + left aside; instead the page content sits inside the main shell with an AntD `<Breadcrumb>` at the top.
No `5 px` (broken space-in-shadow) literal survives in `projectDetail.tsx`.
Visiting `/projects/:projectId` still redirects to `/projects/:projectId/board`.
New / updated tests pass; existing Jest suite is green for the touched files.
i18n keys added under `microcopy.breadcrumb.*` (or equivalent) with `en` + `zh-CN` parity.
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- `npm install` if the env is fresh.

## Automated
- Run `CI=true npm test -- --watchAll=false --runInBand src/pages/projectDetail src/components/breadcrumb`.
- Run `npm run eslint -- --max-warnings 0` on the touched files.
- Run `npx tsc --noEmit`.

## Manual
- Optional: `npm run dev` + visit `/projects/<id>` and `/projects/<id>/board`; confirm the breadcrumb renders and the redirect still works.

## Gotchas
- The `5 px` typo is a hand-typed CSS value bug; do not just remove the trailing `px` — replace the whole shadow declaration with a token.
- Keep AntD `Breadcrumb` v6 syntax (`items` prop, not legacy `<Breadcrumb.Item>` children) so it matches the rest of the codebase.

tasks.name: verify-fix-project-detail-shell
tasks.scopedGoal: Independently verify the project-detail shell collapse + breadcrumb + `5 px` typo fix against the target's acceptance criteria.
tasks.brief: Re-run the target's verify recipe on the worker branch. Confirm no `5 px` literal survives in `projectDetail.tsx`, the breadcrumb renders with anchor entries + `aria-current="page"`, the legacy 2-column grid + aside is gone, the `/projects/:id` redirect still resolves to `.../board`, and i18n parity exists in both locale files. Emit `unit-test-verified` if the Jest run passes against the new test plus a focused targeted test. Treat env failures as `verifier-blocked` and surface the specific failure.
acceptance: Verifier confirms `5 px` typo is gone and breadcrumb is keyboard / screen-reader accessible.
Verifier confirms the redirect to `.../board` survives the shell collapse.
Verifier records a strong Verification class (`unit-test-verified` or `live-ui-verified`).

tasks.name: chat-drawer-polish
tasks.scopedGoal: Close ui-todo §12 chat-drawer items: hide raw `tool` payloads behind a `Show details` toggle, add per-message timestamps + a copy-to-clipboard button on assistant turns, and surface a character-count hint in the prompt input. Preserve every existing aria-label string and the existing autonomy selector wiring.
tasks.brief:
Implementation plan:

1. **`src/components/aiChatDrawer/index.tsx`**: replace the current `<pre>` rendering for `tool` messages with a collapsed-by-default "Show details" toggle (AntD `<Collapse>` or a `Button type="link"` toggling an internal panel). The raw payload stays available, but is not painted in front of users by default (closes ui-todo §12 chat-drawer + 2.A.8 "hide raw tool-call payloads").
2. Add a short timestamp on each message (use `Intl.DateTimeFormat(..., { hour: 'numeric', minute: 'numeric' })`; do NOT use `dayjs.format(...)` for localizable times per 2.A.6).
3. Add an icon-only `Copy` button (AntD `<Button type="text" icon={<CopyOutlined />} aria-label={microcopy.ai.copyMessage}>`) on each assistant message that copies the rendered text to the clipboard via `navigator.clipboard.writeText`. On success, briefly toggle the icon to `CheckOutlined` or surface a transient `message.success` toast.
4. Add a character-count hint beneath the prompt input (e.g. `{count} / {max}`); when `count > max * 0.9` render in `Typography.Text type="warning"`. Use an env-driven or constant cap; default 4000 characters. Do not block submission — purely a hint.
5. Continue to honor the `useDelayedFlag(loading, 250)` spinner throttling (do not regress §16e Phase 3.5 closure).
6. i18n: add `microcopy.ai.toolDetailsToggle`, `microcopy.ai.copyMessage`, `microcopy.ai.copyMessageCopied`, `microcopy.ai.characterCountTemplate` (`{count}/{max}`); en + zh-CN parity.
7. Tests: `src/components/aiChatDrawer/index.test.tsx` (or a new `*.strict.test.tsx`) — assert tool payload is hidden by default; toggle reveals it; assistant message has an accessible copy button; character-count hint switches to the warning class above the threshold. Maintain the existing jest-axe coverage (no new violations).

Do NOT touch `useAgent`, `useAgentChat`, or `useAgentChat.dismissNudge` semantics. Hook destructuring is fine, but do not change the hook API.

UI bug: capture a before/after screenshot or recording of the chat drawer if available; mention the artifact path in your handoff.
acceptance: `<pre>` rendering of tool payloads is gone from the default chat view; details are accessible only behind a toggle with an accessible name.
Each assistant message renders a timestamp via `Intl.DateTimeFormat` and an accessible copy button that writes the rendered text to the clipboard.
Prompt input shows a character-count hint that switches to the warning style above 90% of the cap.
i18n parity (`en` + `zh-CN`) for every new visible string; existing aria-labels are unchanged.
Jest tests pass for `aiChatDrawer`; jest-axe assertions remain clean.
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- `npm install` if needed.

## Automated
- `CI=true npm test -- --watchAll=false --runInBand src/components/aiChatDrawer src/__tests__/aiAccessibility`.
- `npm run eslint -- --max-warnings 0` on touched files.
- `npx tsc --noEmit`.

## Manual
- Optional: `npm run dev`, open the chat drawer, confirm tool payloads collapsed by default, copy/timestamps/char-count working.

## Gotchas
- Some downstream tests inspect raw text content of assistant messages — make sure timestamp strings are matchable or wrapped in a stable element with a `data-testid` so the existing tests still pass.
- `navigator.clipboard` is undefined in jsdom; in tests, stub `Object.assign(navigator, { clipboard: { writeText: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue() } })`.

tasks.brief: Re-run the target's verify recipe on the worker branch. Confirm tool payloads no longer render in the default view (only behind the toggle), assistant messages expose a labeled copy button, the character-count hint is wired, and `aiAccessibility.strict.test.tsx` passes without new jest-axe violations. Emit `unit-test-verified` on a clean Jest pass; treat env failures as `verifier-blocked`.
acceptance: Verifier confirms raw tool JSON is hidden by default and accessible only behind the toggle.
Verifier confirms copy button has an accessible name and the character-count hint is keyed in `microcopy.ai.*`.
Verifier records a strong Verification class.

tasks.name: auth-form-completion
tasks.scopedGoal: Close ui-todo §11 + Phase 2.7 remaining: add a top-of-form error summary (GOV.UK pattern, WCAG 3.3.1 / 3.3.3) with anchor links to failing fields on both login and register; audit `inputMode` / `enterKeyHint` / `onPaste` per 2.A.1 (no paste blocking, correct soft-keyboard hints); make the auth card adapt to viewport (`max-width: 40rem; width: min(40rem, 100% - 2rem)`); replace the `NoPaddingButton type="link"` register-CTA on login (and the inverse on register) with an AntD `<Link>` so the semantic role stops being muddied.
tasks.brief:
Implementation plan:

1. **Error summary**: introduce a new `src/components/authErrorSummary/index.tsx` (or co-locate inside `loginForm` and `registerForm` if simpler) that renders a `role="alert"` block above the form whenever the API returns a structured error or `Form.useForm().getFieldsError()` reports any failing fields after submit. Each anchor links via `<a href="#field-id">` to the corresponding field; clicking moves focus. Use `microcopy.auth.errorSummary*` keys.
2. **Input attributes**: ensure every `<Form.Item>` input has:
   - `inputMode` (`email` for email; `text` for username/password).
   - `enterKeyHint="go"` on the password (last field before submit) and `enterKeyHint="next"` on earlier fields.
   - DO NOT block paste on password fields; remove any `onPaste={(e) => e.preventDefault()}` if present.
3. **Adaptive width**: in `src/layouts/authLayout.tsx`, change the card sizing from a hard-coded `40rem`/`56rem` to `max-width: 40rem; width: min(40rem, 100% - 2rem)`; remove or simplify the absolutely positioned background SVG `calc()` math that collapses on small screens (replace with a subtle gradient or a `max-width` constrained image).
4. **Register / login CTA semantics**: replace the bottom `NoPaddingButton type="link"` ("Register for an account" / "Already have an account") with a real AntD `<Link>` (react-router) or `Typography.Link` so the role muddiness with the column "..." menu / header logout is gone.
5. i18n: every new visible string keyed under `microcopy.auth.*` (errorSummary title + body, ARIA region labels); `en` + `zh-CN` parity.
6. Tests: extend `src/components/loginForm/*.test.tsx` and `src/components/registerForm/*.test.tsx`:
   - Submitting with empty required fields renders the error summary with anchor links to each failing field.
   - Clicking an anchor moves DOM focus to the matching field.
   - Paste on password fields is not blocked.
   - Adaptive width: jsdom `getComputedStyle` confirms `width: min(...)` via inline style or a class that resolves to the right rule.
   - Keep existing tests green (caps-lock hint, password strength, etc.).

Do NOT modify any AI surface or any non-auth page. Capture a before/after screenshot of the login form at 320 px and ≥ 1024 px viewports if you can.
acceptance: Login + register render a top-of-form error summary (`role="alert"`) with anchor links to failing fields after submit.
Auth inputs declare the right `inputMode` / `enterKeyHint`; password fields do NOT block paste.
Auth card adapts to small viewports (no overflow at 320 px width); decorative SVG math no longer collapses.
Register-CTA on login (and inverse on register) is a real `<Link>`, not a faux `NoPaddingButton type="link"`.
i18n parity for new strings; tests pass; existing auth tests stay green.
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- `npm install`.

## Automated
- `CI=true npm test -- --watchAll=false --runInBand src/components/loginForm src/components/registerForm src/components/authErrorSummary`.
- `npm run eslint -- --max-warnings 0` on touched files.
- `npx tsc --noEmit`.

## Manual
- Optional: `npm run dev`, open `/auth/login` and `/auth/register`; submit empty forms; confirm error summary renders, anchors move focus, and the card looks sensible at small widths.

## Gotchas
- AntD `Form.useForm()` returns errors via `getFieldsError()`; subscribe via `<Form.Item shouldUpdate>` or `Form.useWatch` to keep the summary reactive without re-render storms.
- `min()` CSS isn't supported in IE11 (we don't target IE; fine for current browsers).

Emit `unit-test-verified` on a green run; surface concrete failures otherwise.
acceptance: Verifier confirms the error-summary, paste-policy, adaptive-width, and CTA-semantics rows are met.
Verifier records a strong Verification class.

tasks.name: dnd-affordances
tasks.scopedGoal: Close ui-todo §15 + Phase 3.8 microinteractions: add a styled drop placeholder (dashed border + tinted background), a visible card lift (`box-shadow` + slight `scale`) during drag start, and a column drag-handle so the entire column is no longer the drag target. Honor `prefers-reduced-motion` so the lift / placeholder animation has zero motion when the OS prefers reduced motion. Keep the existing keyboard-drag hint (`microcopy.dragHints.taskCardKeyboard`) intact.
tasks.brief:
Implementation plan:

1. **`src/components/column/index.tsx`** (and any styled components co-located there):
   - Task card lift on `isDragging`: the `<Draggable>` render prop already exposes `snapshot.isDragging`; apply a `transform: scale(1.02)` and `box-shadow: var(--lift-shadow)` (sourced from `src/theme/tokens.ts` `shadow.lift` or similar) while `isDragging` is true.
   - Drop placeholder: target `[data-rfd-placeholder-context-id]` (the placeholder injected by `@hello-pangea/dnd`) with a `1px dashed colorPrimary` border and a subtle tint (`rgba(var(--rgb-primary), 0.08)` or token-based) so users see where the card will land.
   - Column drag-handle: introduce a small handle (e.g. `<HolderOutlined />`) in the column header that is the only `Draggable.dragHandleProps` target for the column; the rest of the column header is no longer the drag handle.
   - Wrap any non-zero motion in `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)` so reduced-motion users get an instant snap (per 2.A.3 / WCAG 2.3.3).
2. **`src/components/column/index.test.tsx`** (or a new `column-dnd.test.tsx`):
   - Snapshot or RTL test asserting the lift class / inline shadow is applied when `snapshot.isDragging` is true, and absent otherwise.
   - Test asserting the column drag-handle has an accessible name (`aria-label={microcopy.dragHints.columnDragHandle}` or equivalent) and that `dragHandleProps` are spread only on the handle, not the whole column.
3. i18n: add `microcopy.dragHints.columnDragHandle` (and any new visible string); `en` + `zh-CN`.
4. Verify existing tests (especially the keyboard-drag hint test in `src/components/column/index.test.tsx`) stay green.

Do NOT modify `useDragEnd.ts`, the reorder mutation logic, or the AGENTS.md-tracked `disableInteractiveElementBlocking` pattern; the data side already works.

Capture a short before/after recording of a card being dragged if a dev environment is available; mention the artifact path in your handoff.
acceptance: Dragging a task card visibly lifts (shadow + slight scale) while the drag is active; reverts when the drag ends.
Drop placeholder is styled (dashed border + tinted background); no more bare/invisible placeholder.
Column drag-handle is a small explicit handle in the column header; the rest of the column is not the drag target.
All motion respects `prefers-reduced-motion`.
Existing keyboard-drag hint test stays green; new tests pass.
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- `npm install`.

## Automated
- `CI=true npm test -- --watchAll=false --runInBand src/components/column`.
- `npm run eslint -- --max-warnings 0` on touched files.
- `npx tsc --noEmit`.

## Manual
- Optional: `npm run dev`, drag a card between columns; confirm the lift, placeholder, and column drag-handle. Toggle the OS reduced-motion setting and re-test for instant snap behavior.

## Gotchas
- `@hello-pangea/dnd` re-renders on every drag event; avoid styling via `style={{ ... }}` per-render — prefer a CSS class toggle to keep CPU costs predictable.
- The library blocks drags whose event target is a native interactive element (see AGENTS.md). When making the column header drag-handle a `<button>`, pass `disableInteractiveElementBlocking` on the column's `<Draggable>` (or `<Drag>`) as documented.

tasks.name: verify-dnd-affordances
tasks.scopedGoal: Independently verify the drag-and-drop affordances (card lift, drop placeholder, column drag-handle, reduced-motion guard).
tasks.brief: Re-run the target's verify recipe on the worker branch. Confirm `isDragging` triggers the lift class / inline shadow; the column drag-handle is a focusable element with an accessible name; the existing keyboard-drag-hint regression test still passes; reduced-motion media query gates the motion. Emit `unit-test-verified` on a clean run.
acceptance: Verifier confirms card lift + drop placeholder + column drag-handle land per the acceptance.
Verifier confirms `prefers-reduced-motion` honors the no-motion path.
Verifier records a strong Verification class.

tasks.name: feedback-parity
tasks.scopedGoal: Close ui-todo §20b: extend `AiFeedbackPopover` parity to `AiTaskAssistPanel` suggestions (the suggested story-points / readiness card) and `BoardBriefDrawer` recommendations, so users can leave thumbs-up / thumbs-down feedback on every AI-surfaced suggestion — not just chat turns. Reuse the existing `AiFeedbackPopover` analytics payload shape and keep the chat drawer wiring untouched.
tasks.brief:
Implementation plan:

1. **`src/components/aiTaskAssistPanel/index.tsx`**: mount `AiFeedbackPopover` next to the suggested story-points + readiness card, scoped to the active suggestion id (or composite of `(taskName, suggestionRunId)` if no id is available). Pass through `surface="task-assist"` (or equivalent) into the feedback payload. Respect `useDelayedFlag(loading, 250)` so the feedback control doesn't flash while the suggestion is still loading.
2. **`src/components/boardBriefDrawer/index.tsx`**: mount `AiFeedbackPopover` on the structured recommendation block (per `recommendationDetail`); scope by `(brief_run_id || lastSuggestion.id)` if available, else by `(projectId, runStartedAt)`. Surface `surface="board-brief"` in the payload.
3. **`src/components/aiFeedbackPopover/index.tsx`**: extend the existing surface enum / typing if needed to accept the two new surfaces. Do not change the existing chat usage.
4. i18n: reuse `microcopy.feedback.*` if it already covers the parity strings; otherwise add `microcopy.feedback.taskAssistTitle` / `boardBriefTitle` with `en` + `zh-CN` parity.
5. Tests: extend `aiAccessibility.strict.test.tsx` (or the per-component test files) to assert the popover renders with the right ARIA name on each surface and that submitting feedback fires the existing analytics event (`AGENT_FEEDBACK_SUBMITTED` or whatever the current event id is).
6. Capture a screenshot of the feedback popover on each surface if a dev env is available.

Do NOT modify any analytics sink wiring (`httpAnalyticsSink`, `devMemorySink`) — reuse the existing event shape.
acceptance: `AiFeedbackPopover` is mounted next to the AiTaskAssistPanel suggestion card and the BoardBriefDrawer recommendation block.
Submitting feedback on each surface fires the existing analytics event with `surface="task-assist"` and `surface="board-brief"` respectively.
i18n parity (`en` + `zh-CN`) for any new strings; existing chat-drawer feedback usage is unchanged.
Tests pass on the touched components; jest-axe assertions remain clean.
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- `npm install`.

## Automated
- `CI=true npm test -- --watchAll=false --runInBand src/components/aiTaskAssistPanel src/components/boardBriefDrawer src/components/aiFeedbackPopover src/__tests__/aiAccessibility`.
- `npm run eslint -- --max-warnings 0` on touched files.
- `npx tsc --noEmit`.

## Manual
- Optional: `npm run dev`; open the task modal AI assist + board brief; submit feedback; confirm analytics event in dev memory sink.

## Gotchas
- The chat drawer already mounts the popover with a per-message id; mirror that pattern but key the popover on `(suggestion.id, surface)` so duplicate suggestions on the same task can be rated independently.
- Do NOT regress 2.A.7 throttled spinners; the popover should not render at all while `isLoading` is true.

tasks.name: docs-status-hygiene
tasks.scopedGoal: Close the docs/status loop for the five implementation workers: strike or mark complete the matching entries in `docs/status/ui-todo.md` and add one-liner rows under `docs/status/product-done.md` per AGENTS.md backlog hygiene. Do NOT modify code outside `docs/status/**` (no further i18n/test/component edits).
tasks.brief:
Implementation plan:

1. Read each upstream handoff (pasted into this prompt by the orchestrator) and pull out:
   - the closed item(s) (e.g. `ui-todo §5`, `ui-todo §12 chat-drawer`, `ui-todo §11 / Phase 2.7 remaining`, `ui-todo §15`, `ui-todo §20b`),
   - the branch the worker pushed to,
   - the evidence (test file paths, key file paths).
2. **`docs/status/ui-todo.md`**: for each closed item, edit the existing bullet to **strike** the resolved sub-bullets (preserve the strike-through convention used throughout the doc) and add a `**[Complete on branch `orch/todo-sweep-566b/<task-name>`: <evidence>.]**` annotation. Do NOT delete items wholesale unless every sub-bullet in the row is now resolved.
3. **`docs/status/product-done.md`**: append a new row to the `At a glance` table (or whichever section is the right home) for each closed item, with the standard `Surface | PRD section | Status` shape used elsewhere in the file. Reference branch + the closed item ids.
4. Do NOT edit `docs/status/release-todo.md` — GA §1 stays open and is explicitly out of scope for this sweep.
5. Do NOT add tests or run any test command (this worker only edits docs). Run `git diff --stat docs/status/` before pushing as a sanity check.
6. Verify against AGENTS.md: "Don't restate blockers inline in README.md / backend/README.md" — keep this worker scoped to `docs/status/**` only.

If any upstream handoff reports `Status: blocked` or `Status: partial`, do NOT mark its item as complete — instead, add a short note under the existing row explaining what is still open.
acceptance: `docs/status/ui-todo.md` has each successfully-closed item bullet struck-through / marked complete with branch + evidence references.
`docs/status/product-done.md` has a new one-liner row per successfully-closed item.
`docs/status/release-todo.md` is untouched.
If any upstream handoff was `partial` / `blocked`, the matching status row notes what remains open instead of being marked closed.
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- (no install required)

## Automated
- `git diff --stat docs/status/` — only `ui-todo.md` and `product-done.md` should be in the diff.
- `git diff docs/status/release-todo.md` must be empty.

## Manual
- (no UI changes)

## Gotchas
- The branch names recorded in the closed-item rows must match the actual handoff `## Branch` line, not the placeholder `orch/<rootSlug>/<task>` if the worker pushed a different name. Always use the handoff body's `## Branch` value verbatim.
- Don't introduce new tracking documents (per AGENTS.md, "ask before creating a new tracking doc").
